svg.text: Kaiser sweep needs enough FFT density

text.tspan: This sidecar reruns the same Kaiser family at length 129, but compares a coarse 512-point spectral probe against a denser 4096-point one.
ENBW and scalloping stay fixed; the drift lives in the sampled sidelobe and main-lobe measurements.

svg.text: Peak sidelobe level drifts under coarse zero padding

text.tspan: The denser probe shows where the coarse spectrum grid

svg.text: Main-lobe width drifts under coarse padding

text.tspan: The same coarse probe also makes the higher-beta windows
look wider than the denser read does.

svg.text: How much the sidelobe read is off

text.tspan: By β=5.0, the coarse probe is already at least 1 dB off.

svg.text: How much the width read is off

text.tspan: By β=7.5, the coarse probe is already at least half a bin
At β=8.6, the coarse 512-point probe makes the Kaiser window look 4.9 dB cleaner and 1.20 bins wider than the denser 4096-point read. By β=14.0, the coarse read is off by 9.3 dB
and 1.89 bins. ENBW and scalloping do not move at all across this audit, which is the point: the window did not change, only the sampled spectral probe did.
